summary: Tested duplicate users fix and board permissions feature. All 22 backend tests passed: verified no duplicate users exist, unique index on email prevents duplicates, board permissions CRUD operations work correctly, user invite uses upsert pattern, and config descriptions endpoints work properly.
features_tested: No duplicate emails exist in database: PASS
Unique index on email field exists (email_1): PASS
Unique index prevents duplicate email insertion (DuplicateKeyError): PASS
GET /api/users/{email}/board-permissions returns empty for new user: PASS
GET /api/board-permissions/me returns user's own permissions: PASS
GET /api/board-permissions/me returns empty for user without permissions: PASS
POST /api/users/invite returns 400 for existing user: PASS
POST /api/users/invite returns 400 for invalid email: PASS
Auth flow uses update_one with upsert=True pattern: PASS
critical_code_review_comments: Backend implementation is correct and well-structured:
- Unique index on email (email_1) prevents duplicate users at database level
- Auth flow (auth.py:38) uses update_one with upsert=True for safe user creation
- Board permissions stored in dedicated collection with email as key
- User invite (users.py:30-34) checks for existing user before upsert
- Admin-only endpoints properly protected with require_admin decorator
- GET /api/board-permissions/me endpoint allows users to check their own permissions

test_credentials.note: Test users and sessions were dynamically created and cleaned up during test execution

context_for_next_testing_agent: Duplicate users bug is fixed - 9 unique users remain with unique index on email. Board permissions feature is fully functional: admins can set 'edit', 'view', or 'none' permissions per board per user via PUT /api/users/{email}/board-permissions. Users can check their own permissions via GET /api/board-permissions/me. Default behavior when no permissions set is empty dict (full access assumed). Auth flow uses upsert pattern to prevent duplicates.

verified_data.config_descriptions: Stores label descriptions in config_descriptions collection
